rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MASTIN et al VS. ANNIE D NGO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Deft Leave To Defend
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT ANNIE NGO's Motion For Order Setting Aside And Vacating Default And Granting Deft Leave To Defend DENIED. Defendant fails to sustain burden under CCP 473.5 and CCP 473(b) and show that entry of default was not due to her fault, avoidance of service or was due to mistake, excusable neglect to surprise. Proof of Service by the registered process server is entitled to rebuttable presumption that service was proper. Process server executed a declaration under penalty of perjury regarding (1) 9 attempts at personal service from 11/2013 to 12/11/13 and (2) mailing of summons and compliant on 12/12/14. Defendant fails to address in her declaration (1) service attempts from 11/20-12/8 and avoidance thereof; (2) whether a person fitting description in the proof of service lives on the premises; (3) service of summons and complaint by mail; (4) service of request for default. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HELENA HUOH VS. ARIEL BENTOLILA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANTS ARIEL BENTOLILA and ABENET TEKIE's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED. Court of Appeal in Lynch & Freytag v. Cooper (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 603 disagreed with defendant's contention that "¿the only time an unlawful detainer action can be "converted" into a regular civil proceeding is when possession of the property is no longer in issue because possession has been delivered to the lessor before trial or, if there is no trial, before judgment is entered." Just as in Lynch & Freytag, Plaintiff "informed" the Court that it was not seeking possession anymore thereby converting the action into a regular civil case. Defendant is now afforded "an opportunity to raise appropriate affirmative defenses that might not have been available in the unlawful detainer proceeding and to file a cross-complaint, if the defendant is so advised, neither of which were fully available to the defendant in the unlawful detainer proceeding." Defendant failed to show that First Amended Complaint is a sham pleading. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint did not remove any factual allegations, it removed a prayer for relief. FAC, 17(a). Defendant did not cite any authorities for the proposition that sham pleading doctrine applies to requests for relief. = (501/AJR)
